title: Package Installation
description: Installing dependencies from composer.json/lock or adding new ones via the require command

actors.name: EventDispatcher
actors.description: Fires PRE_INSTALL_CMD, PRE_UPDATE_CMD, PRE_OPERATIONS_EXEC script hooks

use_cases.name: Install from lock/composer.json
use_cases.description: Developer runs `composer install`; reads composer.lock (or composer.json fallback) and installs all dependencies
key_steps: Validate no package arguments passed (redirect to require)
Configure install options (dry-run, prefer-source/dist, dev-mode, optimize-autoloader, audit)

use_cases.name: Add new dependency
use_cases.description: Developer runs `composer require vendor/pkg[:version]`; updates composer.json and installs
key_steps: Locate/create composer.json file; backup composer.json + composer.lock
Register SIGINT/SIGTERM handler → revertComposerFile() on abort
determineRequirements() — resolve package names and version constraints
Optionally prompt to use --dev if package has dev/testing keywords
updateFile() via JsonManipulator — write requirement into require/require-dev key

use_cases.name: Resolve dependency graph
use_cases.description: Solver computes install/update/uninstall operations satisfying all constraints

use_cases.name: Execute package operations
use_cases.description: Performs install/update/uninstall on each package using the appropriate concrete installer

use_cases.name: Generate autoloader
use_cases.description: After package operations, regenerate the autoloader
key_steps: Supports optimize, classmap-authoritative, APCu prefix modes

use_cases.name: Run security audit
use_cases.description: Auditor checks installed packages against security advisories
key_steps: Returns ERROR_AUDIT_FAILED (5) on findings when --audit flag set

use_cases.name: Revert on failure
use_cases.description: On installation failure, restore composer.json and composer.lock to pre-command state
key_steps: If newlyCreated: delete composer.json (and lock if present)
Otherwise: restore composerBackup → composer.json, lockBackup → composer.lock
Triggered on non-zero status from Installer::run() (excluding ERROR_AUDIT_FAILED)

use_cases.name: Dry-run installation
use_cases.description: Simulate install/require without writing files or executing operations
key_steps: RequireCommand: skips updateFile(), cleans up newly-created composer.json after

key_relationships: RequireCommand extends BaseCommand; uses Installer, JsonFile, JsonManipulator
Installer::create(IOInterface, Composer): self — static factory wiring all dependencies
Installer depends_on Solver (dependency resolution in doUpdate)
InstallationManager routes to LibraryInstaller, PluginInstaller, MetapackageInstaller, ProjectInstaller
RequireCommand aggregates JsonFile + JsonManipulator for composer.json mutations
important_notes: InstallCommand reads composer.lock first; falls back to composer.json if no lock present (triggers update mode)
RequireCommand always sets setUpdate(true) on Installer; partial update restricted to listed packages when lock exists
Signal handlers (SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP) in RequireCommand ensure atomic composer.json changes
GC disabled during Installer::run() for performance with large dependency trees
Plugin deactivation occurs before doUpdate() in RequireCommand to avoid stale plugin state
